summary: High‑fidelity Betty‑Board sound captures a tight first set and blissful second set highlighted by stellar performances of Looks Like Rain, Sugaree and St. Stephen.
review: The recording is an excellent Betty‑Board mix with clear, punchy balance; only occasional dropouts appear on Tennessee Jed and Deal. Musically the show shines from a laid‑back yet tight first set into a second‑set flow that gels beautifully, featuring standout renditions of Looks Like Rain, Sugaree, St. Stephen and a soaring version of The Music Never Stops.

ai_rating.rationale: Consistently high star ratings (average ≈4.9) and enthusiastic comments on both performance and sound quality, with only minor technical hiccups.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Very clear Betty‑Board mix; low gain noted but easily corrected. Small dropout glitches reported on Tennessee Jed and Deal, otherwise crisp and well‑balanced.

show_quality.setlist_flow: First set described as long, laid‑back yet tight; second set opens with Bertha and flows into a blissful Scarlet > Fire segment, maintaining high energy throughout.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Praised for perfect note choice and phrasing on the Looks Like Rain solo and overall tone.
band_member_comments.Phil: His bass line on Looks Like Rain called "incredibly melodic and flowing".
band_member_comments.Bob: Noted that cutting Sugar Magnolia a bit short was disappointing, implying his vocal/guitar lead could have extended it.
